benefits.benefit_desc: Rehmannia alleviates inflammation, meaning it may help reduce redness, swelling, pain, and heat in various parts of the body. The main active constituents of Rehmannia glutinosa that contribute to this benefit include iridoid glycosides, such as catalpol and acteoside, as well as phenylethanoid glycosides, like rehmaglutins A and B. The primary parts of the Rehmannia glutinosa plant that possess this anti-inflammatory property are the roots, particularly when they are harvested after one to two years of growth. The main medicinal preparations of Rehmannia glutinosa that provide this benefit are decoctions, extracts, and powdered forms, often found in traditional Chinese medicine formulas. This benefit of Rehmannia glutinosa helps relieve various health conditions, such as arthritis, dermatitis, and other inflammatory-related disorders, by potentially inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes.

Rehmannia regulates blood sugar levels, meaning it helps to maintain normal blood glucose levels in the body. This is achieved through the presence of active constituents such as catalpol, stachyose, and verbascoside, which are found in the roots of the plant. The primary parts of the Rehmannia glutinosa plant that provide this benefit are the roots, which contain the highest concentration of these active compounds. The main medicinal preparations of Rehmannia glutinosa that provide this benefit include decoctions, extracts, and powdered forms of the root. This benefit of Rehmannia glutinosa is particularly helpful in relieving health conditions such as diabetes, hyperglycemia, and metabolic disorders, making it a valuable tool in the management of blood sugar-related health issues.

Rehmannia boosts immune system, meaning it helps strengthen the body's natural defense against foreign substances, such as bacteria, viruses, and parasites. The main active constituents of Rehmannia glutinosa that contribute to this benefit are iridoid glycosides, such as catalpol and acteoside, as well as phenylethanoid glycosides, such as rehmaglutin A and B. The primary parts of the Rehmannia glutinosa plant that provide this benefit are the roots, which contain the highest concentration of active compounds. The main medicinal preparations of Rehmannia glutinosa that provide this benefit are decoctions, extracts, and powdered forms, which can be taken orally or used topically. This benefit of Rehmannia glutinosa helps relieve various health conditions, including autoimmune disorders, chronic fatigue syndrome, and recurrent infections.

Rehmannia improves liver function, meaning it aids in the proper regulation and maintenance of the organ's state, allowing it to perform its metabolic, detoxifying, and protective roles effectively. The main active constituents of Rehmannia glutinosa that contribute to this benefit are iridoid glycosides, such as catalpol and acteoside, as well as phenylethanoid glycosides and various alkaloids. The primary parts of the Rehmannia glutinosa plant that provide this benefit are the roots, particularly the dried and processed roots known as "Dihuang" in traditional Chinese medicine. The main medicinal preparations of Rehmannia glutinosa that give this liver function benefit include decoctions, powdered extracts, and liquid extracts, often found in traditional herbal formulations. This health benefit of Rehmannia glutinosa helps relieve various liver-related health conditions, such as liver damage, hepatitis, and chemical-induced liver injury, by protecting liver cells, reducing inflammation, and promoting liver cell regeneration.

Rehmannia supports kidney health, meaning it helps maintain the proper function and overall well-being of the kidneys. The main active constituents of Rehmannia glutinosa that contribute to this benefit are iridoid glycosides, such as catalpol and acteoside, as well as phenylethanoid glycosides and oleanane-type triterpenes. The primary parts of the Rehmannia glutinosa plant that provide this benefit are the roots, which contain the highest concentration of beneficial compounds. The main medicinal preparations of Rehmannia glutinosa that give this benefit include decoctions, powdered extracts, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas or added to other remedies. This benefit of Rehmannia glutinosa helps relieve various health conditions related to kidney function, such as kidney disease, kidney deficiency, and kidney-related symptoms such as proteinuria, hematuria, and edema.

Rehmannia eases arthritis symptoms, meaning it can help alleviate pain, inflammation, and stiffness associated with the condition. The main active constituents of Rehmannia glutinosa that contribute to this benefit include iridoid glycosides, such as catalpol and aucubin, as well as phenylethanoid glycosides, such as acteoside and isoacteoside. The primary parts of the Rehmannia glutinosa plant that provide this benefit are the roots, which contain the highest concentration of these active compounds. The main medicinal preparations of Rehmannia glutinosa that give this benefit include dried and powdered root, decoctions, and extracts. This benefit of Rehmannia glutinosa helps relieve the symptoms of osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis, two common types of arthritis that can cause significant pain and disability.

Rehmannia reduces menopause symptoms, meaning it can alleviate various discomforts that women experience during this stage of life. The main active constituents of Rehmannia glutinosa that contribute to this benefit are iridoid glycosides, such as catalpol and atractyroside, as well as phenylethanoid glycosides and various alkaloids. The primary parts of the Rehmannia glutinosa plant that provide this benefit are the roots, which contain the highest concentration of beneficial compounds. The main medicinal preparations of Rehmannia glutinosa that give this benefit include dried root extracts, decoctions, and powdered forms, which are often used in traditional Chinese medicine. This benefit of Rehmannia glutinosa helps relieve various menopause-related health conditions, such as hot flashes, night sweats, mood swings, sleep disturbances, and vaginal dryness.

Rehmannia promotes cardiovascular health, meaning it helps maintain a healthy heart and circulatory system. The main active constituents of Rehmannia glutinosa that contribute to this benefit are iridoid glycosides, such as catalpol and acteoside, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The primary parts of the Rehmannia glutinosa plant that give this benefit are the roots, which contain the highest concentration of these active compounds. The main medicinal preparations of Rehmannia glutinosa that give this benefit include extracts, decoctions, and powdered forms of the root, which are often used in traditional Chinese medicine. This benefit of Rehmannia glutinosa helps relieve various health conditions related to cardiovascular health, such as hypertension, atherosclerosis, and hyperlipidemia.

Rehmannia enhances cognitive function, meaning it aids in improving memory, attention, and overall brain health. The main active constituents of Rehmannia glutinosa that contribute to this benefit are catalpol, rehmannioside A and D, and acteoside. The primary parts of the Rehmannia glutinosa plant that give this benefit are the roots, which contain the highest concentration of active compounds. The main medicinal preparations of Rehmannia glutinosa that give this benefit include extracts, teas, and capsules made from the dried or powdered roots. This benefit of Rehmannia glutinosa helps relieve health conditions such as Alzheimer's disease, dementia, and age-related cognitive decline.

Rehmannia glutinosa inhibits tumor growth, meaning it has the ability to suppress the development and multiplication of cancer cells, thus potentially slowing down or preventing the progression of cancer. The main active constituents of Rehmannia glutinosa that contribute to this benefit are catalpol, rehmaglutin A, and rehmaglutin B. The primary parts of the Rehmannia glutinosa plant that possess this benefit are the roots, which contain the highest concentration of the beneficial compounds. The main medicinal preparations of Rehmannia glutinosa that provide this benefit include extracts, decoctions, and powdered forms of the root. This benefit of Rehmannia glutinosa is particularly helpful in relieving health conditions such as various types of cancer, including lung, liver, and colon cancer, by reducing the size and spread of tumors.
